notes: Nature journal Articles: 3000 words main text, 6 display items. Methods section does not count toward word limit. Brief Communications: 1500 words. Adapt target_words if submitting to Nature Methods (3000 + extended methods) or Nature Communications (more lenient). One-sentence summary required.

sections.name: abstract
sections.heading: Abstract
sections.notes: Nature Article abstract is unstructured. Three sentences: what is the problem, what you did, what you found + why it matters. Extremely tight. No citations.

sections.name: introduction
sections.heading: Introduction
sections.notes: Scene-setting for a broad audience (Nature readers span all biology + physical sciences). Do NOT use subheadings. Last sentence states the main conclusion of the paper.

sections.name: results
sections.heading: Results
sections.notes: Narrative flow with short subheadings. Each subheading introduces one main result. Integrated interpretation is acceptable (unlike IMRaD). Refer to figures/tables as primary evidence.

sections.name: discussion
sections.heading: Discussion
sections.notes: Short. Broader significance, caveats, future directions. No repetition of Results. End with a 2-sentence statement of the advance.

sections.name: methods
sections.heading: Methods
sections.notes: Does NOT count toward 3000-word main text limit. Detailed reproducibility: reagents, code, statistical analyses. Online Methods for extended detail.

sections.name: data_availability
sections.heading: Data Availability
sections.notes: Where data are deposited (GEO, Zenodo, Figshare). Required by Nature since 2017.

sections.name: code_availability
sections.heading: Code Availability
sections.notes: URL to public code repository. Required if code was used.

sections.name: references
sections.heading: References
sections.notes: Nature limits to 50 references for Articles. Numbered style [1]. Use only published or in-press work.
